basic_details.job_title: KEA VAO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - 572 Village Accountant Posts, Apply Online
basic_details.post_name: Village Accountant / Grama Aadalitha Adhikari (Village Administrative Officer)
basic_details.short_information: The table below summarizes the key facts of this recruitment for quick reference.

application_fee.details: The application fee must be paid online only, at the time of form submission. Fees once paid are non-refundable under any circumstances.

age_limit.details: The minimum age required is 18 years as on the closing date of application, as per the Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977. The upper age limit for direct recruitment, inclusive of the 5-year relaxation granted vide Government Order dated 29 January 2026, is given below. | Additional age relaxation beyond the above limits is permitted for specific categories, as detailed in the notification, including: | Persons already serving in Karnataka Government, local body, State/Central statutory or PSU service - relaxation equal to years of service or 10 years, whichever is less. Ex-servicemen - relaxation equal to the length of service rendered, plus 3 years. National Cadet Corps (NCC) full-time supervisors - relaxation equal to years of service. Persons employed as Grama Samuha Parishilakaru under the rural self-employment scheme - relaxation equal to years of service. Special Chetana (persons with disabilities) candidates - 10 years relaxation. Persons employed by the Government of India census organization in Karnataka - relaxation equal to years of service or 5 years, whichever is less. Widows who have not remarried, or women legally divorced and not remarried - 10 years relaxation, subject to proof. Retrenched employees who repaid retrenchment compensation as per the Karnataka Retrenched Employees Act/1957 rules - 10 years relaxation, subject to proof. Transgender/third-gender candidates - 1% horizontal reservation with corresponding age relaxation as per the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019. | Candidates seeking any of the above relaxations must produce the original supporting certificate at the time of document verification.

qualification.details: Candidates must satisfy both the nationality and educational qualification requirements laid down in the notification before applying. | The candidate must be a citizen of India. A living man having more than one wife, and a woman who has married such a man, are not eligible for appointment without prior Government permission. Candidates must be mentally and physically fit to discharge the duties of the post. | Candidates applying for the post of Village Accountant must have passed PUC (Pre-University Course, equivalent to Class 12) or a qualification recognized as equivalent to PUC by the Government of Karnataka. Equivalent qualifications include: | Class 12 examination conducted by CBSE or ICSE boards. Class 12 examination conducted by other State Government pre-university education boards. Advanced vocational courses/NSDC-affiliated courses conducted through the National Institute of Open Schooling (NIOS). Candidates with a two-year ITI (Industrial Training Institute) course, provided they have also passed one language subject and one general subject at a pre-degree level examination, are treated as equivalent to PUC only for these specified fields. Candidates who completed a three-year diploma from a technical education department institution before 2015 are also required to have cleared the Kannada-language subject through an SSLC-level examination. | Candidates possessing a qualification higher than PUC are also eligible to apply, provided they meet the other eligibility conditions.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates for the post of Village Accountant will be placed on the state government pay scale below and will be entitled to benefits under the National Pension Scheme (NPS). All appointees will serve a probation period of 2 years from the date of regular appointment. | Pay Scale: ₹34,100 - 800 - 35,700 - 900 - 39,300 - 1,000 - 43,300 - 1,125 - 47,800 - 1,250 - 52,800 - 1,375 - 58,300 - 1,500 - 64,300 - 1,650 - 67,600

selection_process: Selection to the post of Village Accountant is made purely through a competitive written examination; the notification does not mention any separate interview round. The process is as follows:

Candidates will appear for a competitive written examination conducted in Offline-OMR mode. A minimum of 35% marks in the competitive exam is required for eligibility; candidates scoring below this will be disqualified. Candidates opting for the Kannada language exam exemption must have passed Kannada as first or second language up to SSLC level
or must clear a compulsory qualifying Kannada language test (150 marks

minimum 50 marks required to qualify). This paper is not counted toward merit ranking. District-wise merit lists / marks lists will be prepared and forwarded by KEA to the respective District Magistrates for appointment
which is made at the district level.

pattern_table.Subject: General Knowledge (current affairs, general science, Indian Constitution, Indian and Karnataka history and geography, Panchayat Raj and rural cooperative institutions, and Karnataka regional issues and development)
General Kannada, General English, and Computer Knowledge

exam_pattern.details: The written examination consists of two papers, each carrying 100 marks and lasting 2 hours, with negative marking of 0.25 marks for every wrong answer. | Each question offers 4 answer options with only one correct answer. A minimum qualifying score of 35% is required in the competitive exam.

how_to_apply: Applicants must apply strictly online through the KEA CET portal by following the steps below.
Visit the official KEA website: cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea
Click on the application link for VAO Recruitment 2026 applicable to your cadre (General/RPC or Kalyana-Karnataka).
Register with your basic details and generate login credentials.
Fill in the online application form with personal, educational, and category details, selecting only one district of preference.
Upload a recent passport-size photograph (JPEG format, 50KB-200KB) and signature (JPEG format, 50KB-70KB) as per the specified format.
Verify all entered details carefully, as no correction is allowed after submission.
Pay the applicable application fee online and complete the submission.
Download and retain a printout of the submitted application and fee payment receipt for future reference.
important_instructions: Applications will be accepted online only; offline or postal applications will not be considered. Candidates can apply to only one district; applications submitted for more than one district will not be accepted. Original documents must be produced at the time of document verification for eligibility, age, and category claims. Failure to appear with valid original documents on the specified date will lead to cancellation of candidature. Candidates are responsible for reading all eligibility instructions before applying; incomplete or incorrect information may result in rejection of candidature at any stage. Impersonation, submission of forged documents, or use of unfair means during the exam or interview process will lead to disqualification and possible criminal/disciplinary action. Aadhaar Card is mandatory for application submission.

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates should read the full official notification before applying, as it contains complete details on eligibility, reservation, and exam rules. Two separate notifications have been issued for this recruitment cycle - one for the General/RPC cadre (Notification No. ED/KEA/26/NEV/2026(RPC)) and one for the Kalyana-Karnataka cadre (Notification No. ED/KEA/27/NEV/2026(KK)) - both dated 10 July 2026. Download KEA General / RPC Cadre Notification PDF Download KEA Kalyana-Karnataka Cadre Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Important Dates 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must complete the entire application process, including fee payment, within the schedule given below. No changes are permitted after submission, so applicants should keep these dates in mind.

rows.event: Last Date to Pay Application Fee

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: The 572 vacancies are distributed across two separate cadres based on region. Candidates are allowed to apply for only one district within their applicable cadre; multi-district applications will not be accepted. Note: The notification clarifies that vacancy and category-wise figures may be revised by the concerned district/revenue authorities from time to time, and applicants are advised to check the concerned district authority's corrigendum before finalizing their choice of district.

tables.caption: General / RPC Cadre - District-wise Vacancies (505 Posts)

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must satisfy both the nationality and educational qualification requirements laid down in the notification before applying. The candidate must be a citizen of India. A living man having more than one wife, and a woman who has married such a man, are not eligible for appointment without prior Government permission. Candidates must be mentally and physically fit to discharge the duties of the post. Candidates applying for the post of Village Accountant must have passed PUC (Pre-University Course, equivalent to Class 12) or a qualification recognized as equivalent to PUC by the Government of Karnataka. Equivalent qualifications include: Class 12 examination conducted by CBSE or ICSE boards. Class 12 examination conducted by other State Government pre-university education boards. Advanced vocational courses/NSDC-affiliated courses conducted through the National Institute of Open Schooling (NIOS). Candidates with a two-year ITI (Industrial Training Institute) course, provided they have also passed one language subject and one general subject at a pre-degree level examination, are treated as equivalent to PUC only for these specified fields. Candidates who completed a three-year diploma from a technical education department institution before 2015 are also required to have cleared the Kannada-language subject through an SSLC-level examination. Candidates possessing a qualification higher than PUC are also eligible to apply, provided they meet the other eligibility conditions.

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Age Limit 2026
content_sections.text: The minimum age required is 18 years as on the closing date of application, as per the Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules, 1977. The upper age limit for direct recruitment, inclusive of the 5-year relaxation granted vide Government Order dated 29 January 2026, is given below. Additional age relaxation beyond the above limits is permitted for specific categories, as detailed in the notification, including: Persons already serving in Karnataka Government, local body, State/Central statutory or PSU service - relaxation equal to years of service or 10 years, whichever is less. Ex-servicemen - relaxation equal to the length of service rendered, plus 3 years. National Cadet Corps (NCC) full-time supervisors - relaxation equal to years of service. Persons employed as Grama Samuha Parishilakaru under the rural self-employment scheme - relaxation equal to years of service. Special Chetana (persons with disabilities) candidates - 10 years relaxation. Persons employed by the Government of India census organization in Karnataka - relaxation equal to years of service or 5 years, whichever is less. Widows who have not remarried, or women legally divorced and not remarried - 10 years relaxation, subject to proof. Retrenched employees who repaid retrenchment compensation as per the Karnataka Retrenched Employees Act/1957 rules - 10 years relaxation, subject to proof. Transgender/third-gender candidates - 1% horizontal reservation with corresponding age relaxation as per the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019. Candidates seeking any of the above relaxations must produce the original supporting certificate at the time of document verification.

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Salary 2026
content_sections.text: Selected candidates for the post of Village Accountant will be placed on the state government pay scale below and will be entitled to benefits under the National Pension Scheme (NPS). All appointees will serve a probation period of 2 years from the date of regular appointment. Pay Scale: ₹34,100 - 800 - 35,700 - 900 - 39,300 - 1,000 - 43,300 - 1,125 - 47,800 - 1,250 - 52,800 - 1,375 - 58,300 - 1,500 - 64,300 - 1,650 - 67,600

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee must be paid online only, at the time of form submission. Fees once paid are non-refundable under any circumstances.

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Selection to the post of Village Accountant is made purely through a competitive written examination; the notification does not mention any separate interview round. The process is as follows: Candidates will appear for a competitive written examination conducted in Offline-OMR mode. A minimum of 35% marks in the competitive exam is required for eligibility; candidates scoring below this will be disqualified. Candidates opting for the Kannada language exam exemption must have passed Kannada as first or second language up to SSLC level, or must clear a compulsory qualifying Kannada language test (150 marks, minimum 50 marks required to qualify). This paper is not counted toward merit ranking. District-wise merit lists / marks lists will be prepared and forwarded by KEA to the respective District Magistrates for appointment, which is made at the district level.

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Exam Pattern 2026
content_sections.text: The written examination consists of two papers, each carrying 100 marks and lasting 2 hours, with negative marking of 0.25 marks for every wrong answer. Each question offers 4 answer options with only one correct answer. A minimum qualifying score of 35% is required in the competitive exam.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for KEA VAO Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Applicants must apply strictly online through the KEA CET portal by following the steps below. Visit the official KEA website: cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea Click on the application link for VAO Recruitment 2026 applicable to your cadre (General/RPC or Kalyana-Karnataka). Register with your basic details and generate login credentials. Fill in the online application form with personal, educational, and category details, selecting only one district of preference. Upload a recent passport-size photograph (JPEG format, 50KB-200KB) and signature (JPEG format, 50KB-70KB) as per the specified format. Verify all entered details carefully, as no correction is allowed after submission. Pay the applicable application fee online and complete the submission. Download and retain a printout of the submitted application and fee payment receipt for future reference.

content_sections.heading: KEA VAO Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Applications will be accepted online only; offline or postal applications will not be considered. Candidates can apply to only one district; applications submitted for more than one district will not be accepted. Original documents must be produced at the time of document verification for eligibility, age, and category claims. Failure to appear with valid original documents on the specified date will lead to cancellation of candidature. Candidates are responsible for reading all eligibility instructions before applying; incomplete or incorrect information may result in rejection of candidature at any stage. Impersonation, submission of forged documents, or use of unfair means during the exam or interview process will lead to disqualification and possible criminal/disciplinary action. Aadhaar Card is mandatory for application submission.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for KEA VAO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 30 July 2026, and the fee payment deadline is 31 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in KEA VAO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 572 total vacancies - 505 posts under the General/RPC cadre and 67 posts under the Kalyana-Karnataka cadre.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for KEA VAO 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed PUC or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Government of Karnataka.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for KEA VAO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 40 years for General category, 43 years for Category 2A/2B/3A/3B, and 45 years for SC/ST/Category-1, inclusive of applicable relaxation. The minimum age is 18 years.

faq.question: Q5. Can a candidate apply for more than one district?
faq.answer: No. Candidates can apply for only one district within their applicable cadre. Applications for more than one district will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for KEA VAO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹750 for General/2A/2B/3A/3B candidates, ₹500 for SC/ST/Category-1/Ex-Servicemen/Third Gender candidates, and ₹250 for Special Chetana (PwBD) candidates.

faq.question: Q7. When is the KEA VAO written exam scheduled?
faq.answer: The exam for General/RPC cadre districts is scheduled on 4 October 2026, while the exam for Kalyana-Karnataka cadre districts is scheduled on 25 October 2026, in two papers each day.

faq.question: Q8. Is there an interview after the written exam?
faq.answer: The notification specifies selection purely through the competitive written examination and district-wise merit list; it does not mention a separate interview round.

faq.question: Q9. Is a Kannada language paper compulsory?
faq.answer: Candidates who have not studied Kannada as first or second language up to SSLC level must clear a compulsory qualifying Kannada language test with a minimum of 50 out of 150 marks; those exempted based on prior SSLC-level Kannada study do not need to appear for it.

faq.question: Q10. What is the pay scale for the Village Accountant post?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a pay scale of ₹34,100 - ₹67,600, along with National Pension Scheme benefits, and will serve a 2-year probation period.
